currentFocus: Close the executable rebuild Core/headless source truth, then document and verify the playable Godot client without overstating parity or clean-room status.

currentDocsTruth.release: v1.0.9 remains the latest published unsigned portable WinUI ZIP with 949 packaged Lore documents; this source slice does not publish a release.
currentDocsTruth.product: WinUI 3 is the primary preservation/modding GUI. The separate GPL rebuild now has a deterministic headless Core and will use one Godot .NET visual client; Electron, WPF, and the old Python GUI/CLI remain archived references.
currentDocsTruth.winUiFirstUse: CURRENT_CAPABILITIES.md and its byte-identical Lore mirror record the verified three-state setup hierarchy, preservation of temporarily unavailable remembered folders, manual Save Lab availability, shell status/focus ownership, Level 1 headings, compact wrapped source actions, truthful launch/Admin Level preset state, and demand-loaded Media VLC behavior.
currentDocsTruth.filesystemSafety: Save/options and generated asset/package outputs use bounded roots, held identities, guarded writes, final verification, and explicit same-user post-finalization limits.
currentDocsTruth.re: reverse-engineering/ is canonical; static Ghidra closure remains evidence input rather than automatic runtime or rebuild proof.
currentDocsTruth.lore: The 949-document package count proves packaged breadth, not editorial completeness, freshness, provenance/rights review, or broad public-safety approval.
currentDocsTruth.rebuild: The source checkout now has a GPL-3.0-or-later, RE-informed net8 deterministic Core, strict frozen synthetic command tapes, canonical continuation-state plus rolling input/post-step trace hashes, and independent built-in headless goldens. Explicit tapes require an operator trace hash or explicit no-verification mode. It does not yet have the Godot visual client, retail content, gameplay/visual parity, or strict clean-room status. The former recursive static-to-proof backlog is historical evidence, not an active implementation queue. Curated-export accounting includes the rebuild but excludes the real game-generated save baseline: a hash proves identity, not redistribution provenance, and fixture-dependent UI tests skip when it is absent.
currentDocsTruth.curatedSourcePackage: The curated artifact is a self-contained, plain non-Git convenience package over the already-public canonical repo, not a replacement checkout or a private/public legal cut. Package-specific README/CONTRIBUTING front doors omit source-only goals, coordination, local-overlay, and migration authority. The accepted fixture-free shape is 687 selected source files and 692 final files; clean-tree inventory/payload, all local links, standalone active-product tests, notices, and hygiene passed.

activeBlockers: CURRENT_CAPABILITIES.md and reverse-engineering/RE-INDEX.md remain too large for fast contributor orientation.
release/readiness/ and tools/ still need generated front-door indexes before broad movement or deletion.
Lore needs an explicit editorial allowlist, third-party-text inventory, rights/provenance review, and generated canonical projections before another release.
The grouped WinUI action/reaction map still needs a verified control-by-control refresh against the current GUI.
nextSteps: Run the final provenance export from the accepted commit, then push the Core/headless implementation, provenance boundary, current strategy, export contract, and retired proof-chain guidance.
Add and document the pinned Godot .NET playable client with native runtime and screenshot evidence.
Generate contributor front-door indexes before reorganizing large RE, tools, or release trees.
Run the deep Lore and RE quality passes after the runnable rebuild foundation is accepted.
